title: Spotlight | NYU Shanghai
description: Jointly established by New York University and East China Normal University, NYU Shanghai is the third degree-granting campus in NYU global network, joining NYU Abu Dhabi and NYU New York City.
text:
Spotlight
What I’m Working On: Linguist Linmin Zhang on Language Intuition How might linguistics help us view language and human cognition in a new light?
What I'm Working On: Tyler Haupert on Fintech, Inequality, and UrbanizationWhat role could tech play in making the housing market more equitable? Ask Professor Tyler Haupert.
Building Dreams and Breaking Barriers: NYU Shanghai Hosts Summer Camp for Migrant Youth42 Shanghai-based migrant youth attended the College and Career Lab (NYUSH CCL) last month.
Parents & Students: We are NYU Shanghai!Over the past four years, parents of international students know to “ask Frank if you need help.”
When Your Mom is Your SchoolmateLawrence Lim ’25 probably didn’t expect that his mom, Adele, would soon become his schoolmate.
Course Spotlight: The Graphic NovelStudents learned how to tell a personal story through image and text in comic form.
Global Citizenship, Elite Education, Migration: Tu Siqi’s Social ExplorationAs a sociologist, I’m driven by a curiosity about society and a desire to understand the societal change.
Zhou Xin on VINS: From Risk Research to Real-World ApplicationsThe Volatility Institute at NYU Shanghai (VINS) was established to examine the dynamic China market.
Postdoctoral Fellow Chen Jing: Mapping of Brain Function for Perception and Action“Our ultimate goal is to map the brain throughout the entire lifespan."
What I’m Working On: Alice Wang on Capturing the Nexus of the Real and the Imaginary Professor Alice Wang's solo project Quantum Dream Machine is in this year's Art Basel Hong Kong.
Wu Xiaogang on CASER: Social Science Research for a Better Society"CASER is dedicated to advancing social and economic research in China for the betterment of society."
NYU Shanghai Women’s Voices: Speaking On Strength, Role-Models, and Aspirations"I look up to the female professors at this university. They are like heroes!”
What I’m Working On: Hyoungee Kong Investigates Visual Culture through a Queer LensLearn about Hyoungee Kong, Assistant Professor Faculty Fellow of Art History and her research.
Dialogue with Co-Director Keith Ross: Harnessing the Power of Data Science and AI"New technologies are here to stay and that they will inevitably shape the world we live in."
Sparking Curiosity and Confidence in the Classroom: Zhang Meng on Teaching English For Academic PurposesIn a 5-minute podcast Zhang Meng describes how her EAP course has evolved over the years.
